name: Snowflake
description: Cloud data platform providing data warehouse, data lake, and data application capabilities; exposes a REST SQL API plus SDKs and drivers to run SQL and manage objects programmatically.

auth_methods.note: The SQL API documentation explicitly describes OAuth 2.0 and key-pair (JWT/private-key) authentication and usage of bearer tokens. 'api_key' and 'token' included as general labels because the docs show use of bearer tokens (access tokens) and key-pair credentials registered for API Integration. See the cited page for exact supported flows.

credential_access.note: Snowflake offers a public free trial sign-up flow allowing developers to create accounts and obtain credentials without human approval; combined with docs showing how to create API_INTEGRATION objects inside an account, this supports self-serve credential setup.

api_surface.note: The SQL API docs state the SQL API is a REST API for sending SQL statements over HTTPS. Because the SQL API executes SQL statements, it can access and manage a wide range of Snowflake objects via SQL, so breadth is 'broad'. Additional SDKs and drivers are documented on the developer hub.

buildability.note: Developer-facing REST SQL API docs exist and a public free trial sign-up is available, so a developer can obtain credentials and begin integrating immediately. Production usage may require paid account/consumption (per pricing), but that does not prevent building and testing.

queries: Snowflake developer API documentation root docs.snowflake.com API
